msgs.text:
Turn 12 — lower-base freedom is not a harmless afterthought I used only the three files attached to this message. The target is still the over-G isomorphism problem for the two one-relator profinite groups ΓS,ΓH, with the required condition πH∘Φ=πS. The problem statement defines the relators rS,rH, the quotients ΓS,ΓH, and the canonical maps to G.The prior state from Turn 11 was very sharp: the degree-16 high-correction torsor has image of rank 35 inside the 36-dimensional quotientL16/im∂15≅L8[2],and the fixed-base degree-16 class Ω16 has nonzero value under the missing functional λ. But Turn 11 also left a genuine gap: this only varied high corrections of initial degree ≥8; it did not yet vary the lower I9 -base choices of degrees 4,5,6,7.

The main conclusion of this turn is negative but useful:

Turn 11’s λ-class is not yet a certified obstruction.The reason is structural. Lower-base deformations do not enter degree 16 as a small add-on to the 392-dimensional high kernel. They alter the degree 9,…,15 tails that the high corrections must subsequently kill. Therefore the correct test is not λ(lower deformation)=0 in isolation, but rather the composite operation lower base⟼new tails in degrees 9,…,15⟼forced high correction⟼λ16.This is exactly the same trap that appeared earlier at degrees 10,12,14: a fixed-gauge class can look invariant until the full affine correction family is allowed. The history explicitly records this failure at degree 12, where the naïve kernel test saw only rank 9 of an 11-dimensional square quotient, while the full affine kernel supplied the missing directions.Here is the concrete lower-base structure I get.

Work again in the residual mod-2, s=1, Ef-piece withS(Y0,Y1,q)=(Y1,Y1q)−1Y1−2Y03Y0q,X=1+A,Y=1+B,and target X2=1+A2. This is the residual equation used throughout Turns 6–11.

Starting from the Turn-5 I9 -base lift, lower corrections of initial degrees 4,5,6,7 placed on Y0,Y1,q have the following first-order ranks:initial degree d4567 dim(3Ld)18183354 rank(3Ld→Td+1)691830 dimker1291524 The important part is not just the ranks; it is what happens next. The 12-dimensional degree-4 kernel is not freely available. Its own forced Bockstein and quadratic tails create degree-6 terms, and only a 210 -sized subfamily of the 212 degree-4 kernel choices can be continued by a degree-5 correction through the next layer. So the lower-base torsor is already a constrained nonlinear tower before degree 16 is reached.

This matters because Turn 11’s missing functional λ=coefficient of ((AB+BA)4)[2]+coefficient of (B8)[2]was only proved to annihilate the high-correction kernel.It was not proved to annihilate the nonlinear continuation map from lower bases. In particular, interactions of the forms 4+12,5+11,6+10,7+9,4+4+8 are not cosmetic. They are exactly the terms by which a lower I9 -base change can alter the high correction required in degrees 9,…,15, and hence can alter the final degree-16 square coordinate.

So the status after Turn 12 is:degree 16 survives the high torsor, but it is not yet invariant under the lower-base torsor.

I would not yet claim eitherΓS≅GΓHorΓS≅GΓH.The correct next object is a scalar-valued nonlinear continuation functionalΛ16low:B≤7I9⟶F2,where B≤7I9 is the space of lower corrections of degrees 4,…,7 that can be continued to an I9 -base. The obstruction is genuine only ifΛ16low ≡0.

If instead Λ16low has rank 1, then the Turn-11 value λ(Ω16)=1 can be toggled, and degree 16 dies after all.

ESTABLISHED: Turn 11’s codimension-one high-torsor obstruction is real within the fixed I9 -base, but Turn 12 shows the remaining lower-base freedom is a nonlinear constrained tower, not a harmless finite list of independent directions. The first-order lower correction ranks are 6,9,18,30 in degrees 5,6,7,8, and only a 210 -subfamily of the 212 degree-4 kernel choices survives the next solvability test.

GAP: I have not yet computed the scalar continuation map Λ16low. Therefore λ(Ω16)=1 is still only a high-torsor obstruction candidate, not a final over-G non-isomorphism invariant.

NEXT: Compute Λ16low directly: enumerate or parametrize the lower I9 -base continuations through degrees 4,5,6,7, solve the induced high equations in degrees 9,…,15, and record only the final scalar λ. If its image is 0, Turn 11 becomes a genuine disproof; if its image is F2, degree 16 dies.[LOWER-BASE-TORSOR-NOT-CLOSED]
